findings.evidence: Lines 347-358 manually derive `J' (F v)=0` with `two_smul`, `nth_rewrite`, `abel`, and `sq_apply`, even though lines 256-286 already prove that linear/antilinear maps have the opposite canonical part zero.
findings.fix: Replace the manual `ext v` proof with a short argument using `hlin.complexAntilinearPart_eq_zero`, `hanti.complexLinearPart_eq_zero`, and `complexLinearPart_add_complexAntilinearPart`, or factor it as a reusable lemma `eq_zero_of_isComplexLinear_isComplexAntilinear`.
findings.issue: `isCompl_complexLinearMaps` proves disjointness by a long pointwise algebra block instead of reusing the canonical-part lemmas just proved.

summary: The main proof-quality issue is an overly manual disjointness proof that duplicates algebra already developed earlier in the file and will be brittle under API changes.
